field_mismatches.note: Filing says 'In mid-March, 2013'. Alex picked March 15 as a reasonable mid-March date. Pipeline left date unparsed. Substantively equivalent.

Filing: 'On May 29, 2013, our board of directors held a telephonic special meeting... directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request a best and final proposal from WDC and Company D by May 30, 2013.' The board meeting and directive was May 29. Alex's rough date of 2013-05-28 uses the deadline reference, not the announcement date.
Both agree: '$5.60 per share'. Filing: 'Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share'.
Both classify as Formal. Pipeline enrichment classified evt_019 (mapped to evt_033) as Formal based on inclusion of marked-up merger agreement. Alex agrees.
Both agree: Informal. WDC submitted a 'revised written indication of interest' with a range, after having previously dropped. Pipeline and Alex both classify as Informal.
Both classify as Formal. WDC described it as 'best and final price'. Pipeline enrichment agrees.

Filing: 'the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company F'. The committee made the decision, so DropTarget (target-initiated) is accurate. Pipeline records it as a plain Drop without subtype.
Filing: 'the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E'. DropTarget is correct. Pipeline lacks subtype.

pipeline_only.summary: Board determines to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives.
pipeline_only.note: Alex has no row for this early process event. The pipeline CSV has row 0.7 (Target Sale, 2013-03-26) and row 0.9 (Target Sale, 2013-04-01) which cover the later process steps. This earlier board consideration does not appear in Alex. Filing-grounded via span_0088/span_0089.

pipeline_only.summary: WDC CEO expresses SSD expansion interest at industry lunch.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 0.1 matches this. Alex does not have an equivalent row at this granularity level. Alex's rows start at BidderID 0.5. Filing confirms: 'In 2011, while attending an industry event... Mr. Coyne noted that WDC was interested in exploring potential ways of expanding in SSDs.'

pipeline_only.summary: WDC SVP Hajeck lunch with Moshayedi.
pipeline_only.note: No Alex equivalent. Pipeline captures this as a separate early-interest event. Filing confirms: 'In the summer of 2012, Mr. Moshayedi attended a lunch meeting at the invitation of Mr. Michael Hajeck.'

pipeline_only.summary: Company A's IB contacts sTec management.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 0.2 matches this. Alex does not have a separate row for Company A's initial approach. Filing confirms: 'on November 14, 2012, an investment bank representing Company A contacted sTec's management.'
Pipeline CSV row 0.3 matches this. Alex does not include activist events. Filing confirms.

pipeline_only.summary: Balch Hill files amended 13D urging board action.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 0.4 matches. Alex does not include activist events. Filing confirms.

pipeline_only.summary: Company C expresses interest in particular assets.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 0.6 matches. Alex does not have a row for Company C's interest. Filing confirms: 'On March 13, 2013... representatives of Company C expressed a potential interest in exploring an acquisition of particular assets.'

pipeline_only.summary: Board discusses strategic review, directs outreach.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 0.7 matches. Alex does not have this process step. Filing confirms: 'On March 26, 2013, a regularly scheduled board meeting was held... the board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch.'
Pipeline CSV row 0.9 matches. Alex does not have this outreach-commencement event. Filing confirms: 'On April 1, 2013, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec.'

pipeline_only.summary: Process letter to Company G requesting IOI by May 3.
pipeline_only.note: Pipeline CSV row 5 matches (Final Round Inf Ann, 2013-04-26). Alex has this in the CSV but not in the 28 extracted rows. Filing confirms: 'on April 26, 2013... BofA Merrill Lynch sent a process letter to Company G requesting a non-binding indication of interest by May 3, 2013.'

pipeline_only.summary: Company H contacts BofA expressing interest.
pipeline_only.note: Alex has Company H's NDA (BidderID 11, May 8) but no separate bidder_interest row for Company H's initial contact on May 1. Pipeline captures the two-step (interest then NDA) separately. Filing confirms: 'On May 1, 2013, Company H contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest.'

pipeline_only.summary: WDC verbally reconfirms $9.15 as best possible price.
pipeline_only.note: Alex records only the May 28 written proposal at $9.15 (BidderID 16). Pipeline captures the May 30 verbal reconfirmation as a separate event. Filing confirms: 'On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC's indication of interest to acquire the company at a price of $9.15 per share was WDC's best possible price.' This is a granularity difference -- Alex rolled the verbal reconfirmation into the May 28 proposal.

pipeline_only.summary: WDC communicates it is reevaluating; not prepared to move forward.
pipeline_only.note: Alex has no explicit WDC drop row. Alex jumps from WDC's $9.15 proposal (BidderID 16) to the process rows (16.5-16.7) and then Company D drop (BidderID 17). The pipeline correctly captures this critical WDC withdrawal. Filing confirms: 'On May 31, 2013, a representative of WDC communicated... that WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time.'

Alex has no explicit restart row. Alex's next WDC row is BidderID 18 ($6.60-$7.10 on June 10, labeled Informal), which implicitly represents the re-engagement result. Pipeline explicitly captures the restart event. Filing confirms: 'Over the period from June 1 to June 10, 2013... WDC indicated that a difference of opinion had emerged internally, that WDC's prior bidding approach to an acquisition of sTec had been rejected.'

pipeline_only.summary: Joint press release announcing merger agreement.
pipeline_only.note: Alex has no separate press release row. Alex's Executed row (BidderID 20) covers the execution but not the subsequent announcement. Pipeline captures both. Filing confirms.

alex_only.summary: Alex records a 'Final Round Inf' (informal round completion) spanning 2013-04-23 to 2013-05-03.
alex_only.note: This appears to be Alex's way of recording the completion of the informal IOI round. The pipeline captures the announcement (evt_022 on 4/23) and the deadline (May 3 in the process letter) as structural events rather than a separate 'round' row. This is a granularity/taxonomy difference, not a factual disagreement. Filing confirms the IOI round ran from April 23 announcement to May 3 deadline.

summary.pipeline_coverage: Pipeline has 15 events with no Alex equivalent (all grounded in filing)
summary.alex_coverage: 1 Alex row with no direct pipeline match (Final Round Inf completion -- granularity difference)
top_findings: Pipeline captures WDC drop (May 31) and restart (June 1-10) that Alex omits entirely. These are major timeline events.
Pipeline captures WDC verbal $9.15 reconfirmation (May 30) that Alex folds into the May 28 written proposal.
Alex uses DropTarget subtype for Company F and Company E drops; pipeline records them as plain Drop. Filing supports Alex (target-initiated).
Pipeline has richer pre-process events (WDC 2011 interest, WDC summer 2012, Company A Nov 2012, activist events) that Alex does not track.
Date disagreement on final_round_ext_ann: Alex says 2013-05-28, pipeline says 2013-05-29. Filing supports pipeline (board directive was May 29).
summary.conclusion: Strong agreement on core atomic events. Pipeline provides significantly more granular coverage than Alex, especially around WDC's drop/restart sequence and early-stage interest events. The two substantive gaps in pipeline are: (1) missing DropTarget subtype for Company E and Company F drops, and (2) missing informal round completion marker. Neither is a factual error -- they are taxonomy/granularity differences.
